title: vouacapoua cujabensis
intro: Vouacapoua cujabensis, also known as the Vouacapoua tree, is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of South America, particularly in Brazil. The Vouacapoua cujabensis is known for its medicinal properties, with its roots and stems used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The plant's root bark is used to prepare herbal teas and infusions that have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of fever, inflammation, and digestive problems.
